item.title: v0.2.5
item.description:
First-run setup, local AI, and voice tools

  • Added a first-run setup checklist after macOS permissions.
  • Made Qwen 3.5 2B the default local AI model, with 4B kept as a heavier option for 16 GB+ Macs.
  • Added built-in grammar and copy-text-from-screenshot tests.
  • Improved no-selection Single Shift fallback in text fields that do not expose their content through Accessibility.
  • Added configurable hotkeys, local dictation groundwork, Whisper model management, and experimental OmniVoice text-to-speech controls.
  • Polished onboarding copy, dictionary setup, and release reset commands for testing new-user flows.

item.title: v0.2.4
item.description:
No-selection Shift actions

  • Single Shift can now fix the last sentence before the cursor, even when no text is selected.
  • Double Shift can now flip the last wrong-layout word run before the cursor.
  • No-selection Shift actions are enabled by default and can be disabled in Preferences.
  • Rewrite feedback is more visible: the flip overlay is enabled by default and appears lower on screen.
  • More stable focused-field rewrites preserve surrounding text, blank lines, and cursor position better.
  • Added a built-in short-word rule for Ukrainian-layout бі → Russian бы.
  • Roadmap now tracks user-defined always-flip and never-flip layout rules.

item.title: v0.2.3
item.description:
First-session polish + installable dictionaries

  • Cleaner onboarding with a direct path to permissions, dictionaries, and local AI setup.
  • Simpler menu with direct actions for selected text: flip, fix, translate, and capture text from screen.
  • Installable extended dictionaries for English, Ukrainian, and Russian.
  • Dictionary status now shows active word counts clearly, with update and reset actions.
  • Preferences copy is shorter, calmer, and closer to release quality.
  • Local AI shortcuts enable automatically when the selected Ollama model is installed and ready.
  • Single Shift fixes selected text by default once local AI is ready.
  • Shift+Space translation turns on automatically for a ready local model unless disabled manually.

item.title: v0.2.2
item.description:
Local AI grammar assist + screen-region OCR

  • Single-tap Shift is now the all-purpose AI fix gesture: rewrites the selection if any, else the most recent sentence. Double-tap stays purely mechanical.
  • Screen-region OCR via ⇧⌘S — pick a region, the active vision model drops the recognized text on your clipboard.
  • Auto-fix sentences when you finish a thought with . / ! / ?.
  • Translate selection (⇧Space hotkey + menubar submenu) into a configurable target.
  • OpenAI / OpenRouter / Together / Groq backend (BYOK) alongside Apple Intelligence and Ollama.
  • New onboarding window only appears when permissions are missing — fixes the "looks like the app didn't launch" race.
  • Re-clicking the dock / Applications icon now opens Preferences for an instant visual confirmation.
  • Faster word-flip events: bulk paste + sub-ms gap between backspaces fixes "only the tail of the word flipped" in Slack / Notion / Electron apps.

item.title: v0.2.1
item.description:
Polish + AI scaffold

  • Cross-layout fixes no longer oscillate on real words ("стороны" / "сторони").
  • Standalone "ы" / "э" now correct to "і" / "є" without needing a dictionary lookup.
  • Configurable hotkey: double-tap Shift / right Cmd / right Option.
  • Menu-bar icon replaces the "⌥" placeholder; dims when paused.
  • Refined overlay animation (180° flip, split-spring timing) + Preview button.
  • AI scaffold ready for Apple Intelligence integration (opt-in, dormant by default).
  • README ships with screenshots and animation GIFs.

item.title: v0.2.0
item.description:
First public release

Auto-flip on the fly between EN ↔ UK ↔ RU. Manual hotkey via double-tap Shift (configurable in Preferences). Selection mode for fixing whole sentences. Self-learning Backspace. Cross-layout letter fixes (ы↔і, э↔є). Sticky-shift correction. Per-app blacklist + built-in silence in terminals and password managers. Bouncy icon-flip overlay. Launch at login. Bundled ~45 k UK and ~48 k RU dictionaries.

Signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple. Auto-update via Sparkle from this point forward.
